meta.scene_summary: We are building a future where your AI agent has more access to your root directory than you do to your own electricity.

cooking.headline: Warren Buffett is basically that one developer who refuses to merge until the market crash
cooking.summary: While everyone else is YOLOing into the latest tech hype, Berkshire Hathaway is sitting on a massive pile of cash waiting for the inevitable production outage. It turns out the best investment strategy is just having enough 'local storage' to buy the dip once the server room catches fire.
cooking.plain_english: Berkshire Hathaway has accumulated a large amount of cash while reducing its stock market investments in anticipation of a potential economic downturn.

cooking.headline: Stop giving your AI agents root access to your personal laptop
cooking.summary: Clawk lets you shove your chaotic AI coding agents into a disposable Linux VM instead of letting them roam free on your machine. It turns out that giving an AI access to your actual file system is like letting a toddler drive your car to the grocery store.
cooking.plain_english: Clawk is a tool that allows developers to run automated coding agents in isolated virtual environments rather than on their local computers.

cooking.headline: Anthropic is learning that calling out marketing fluff is the new developer bloodsport.
cooking.summary: The creator of Zig just publicly dismantled Anthropic's messaging, proving that engineers have zero patience for corporate buzzwords. It's like watching a senior dev tear apart a junior's PR because the documentation claims it's 'revolutionary' while the code is clearly just a wrapper.
cooking.plain_english: The developer of the Zig programming language has publicly criticized the marketing claims made by the AI company Anthropic.

cooked.headline: Grok's CLI decided your home directory was a public cloud bucket
cooked.summary: The Grok CLI tool accidentally uploaded entire user home directories to xAI's servers instead of just the intended files. It turns out the best way to ensure your private SSH keys and config files are backed up is to let an AI hallucinate its own upload path.
cooked.plain_english: A command-line tool for the Grok AI service incorrectly uploaded sensitive files from users' personal computer folders to the company's servers.

cooked.headline: Grok's 'helpful' assistant just committed your dotfiles to the cloud
cooked.summary: The Grok CLI tool took the initiative to upload users' entire home directories to xAI's servers without being asked. It is the ultimate 'I helped' moment, assuming your definition of help includes leaking your private environment variables to the internet.
cooked.plain_english: The Grok command-line interface software mistakenly transmitted entire user directories to the developer's servers, exposing private data.

cooked.headline: Ireland is basically just a giant power strip for datacenters now
cooked.summary: Ireland's datacenters are consuming nearly a quarter of the country's electricity, which is a bold strategy for a nation that isn't just one giant server rack. It's impressive how they've managed to turn an entire country into a massive, inefficient heating element for the cloud.
cooked.plain_english: Data centers in Ireland now account for 23% of the country's total electricity consumption.

cooked.headline: The SFPD learned the hard way that 'private' drone footage is an oxymoron
cooked.summary: The San Francisco police department had their drone footage leaked, proving that even the surveillance state has terrible OpSec. It's the digital equivalent of leaving your private group chat open on the projector during a company all-hands meeting.
cooked.plain_english: Leaked drone footage from the San Francisco Police Department has revealed details about their urban surveillance operations.

cooked.headline: Claude Code is burning tokens like it's a student budget on a Friday night
cooked.summary: Claude Code consumes 33,000 tokens just to say hello, effectively charging you for the privilege of waiting for it to notice you. It's the digital equivalent of a group project partner who spends the entire meeting organizing their pens before actually starting the essay.
cooked.plain_english: Claude Code requires a significantly higher amount of data input before beginning a task compared to OpenCode, leading to higher operational costs.

cooked.headline: Math.tanh is now a snitch that tells websites which OS you're using
cooked.summary: Chromium 148 introduced a subtle math precision quirk that allows websites to identify your operating system just by running a single calculation. It's the digital equivalent of someone guessing your home address because you wrote the number seven with a funny little loop at the bottom.
cooked.plain_english: A minor variation in how the math function Math.tanh works in Chromium 148 allows websites to identify the user's operating system for tracking purposes.

cooked.headline: The US financial system is finding out that technical debt isn't just a software problem.
cooked.summary: Economists are sounding the alarm that the global dominance of the dollar is starting to look like a legacy codebase that hasn't seen a refactor in decades. It turns out that relying on 'it works on my machine' works a lot less well when your machine is the entire global economy.
cooked.plain_english: The United States is facing challenges that may threaten the long-standing global dominance of the American dollar.
